steps.title: Rewrite with common denominator
steps.content:
Notice both sides involve the denominator $(x+8)$. Rewrite the right side with the same denominator:
$$1 - \frac{1}{x+8} = \frac{x+8-1}{x+8} = \frac{x+7}{x+8}$$
The equation becomes:
$$\frac{2(x+2)}{x+8} = \frac{x+7}{x+8}$$

steps.title: Equate numerators and solve
steps.content:
Since the denominators are equal, the numerators must be equal:
$$2(x+2) = x+7$$
$$2x + 4 = x + 7$$
$$x + 4 = 7$$
$$x = 3$$
So B is correct.
